content.text:
<user_query>
The homepage outside of the _authed section should have controls to redirect the user to sign in (which is also sign up) which is the auth app, not in this app. Similarly any time we need to logout or refresh the auth token we will hit an auth app endpoint. Therefore we _shouldn't_ need any sign in, sign up, or logout page in this codebase. Lets make a plan for refactoring these out correctly. Ask me questions until you're satisfied we are both on the same page about this work.
</user_query>
I need to dig into the codebase to see how the authentication pages and flow are currently structured.

input.command: ls src src/routes src/components 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; ls src/routes/_authed 2>/dev/null
ls src/routes/_login src/routes/_authed src/routes; echo ---; find src/routes -maxdepth 3 -type f | head -60
ls src/lib src/routes/_authed/index.tsx 2>/dev/null; echo --; find src -maxdepth 3 -name 'oauth*' -o -name '*auth*' 2>/dev/null

content.text: I see the issue now — the homepage is currently nested inside the `_authed` layout, which means the root route `/` is auth-gated. The user needs a public landing page at `/` outside of authentication, with buttons to direct users to sign in or sign up, and they also want logout functionality handled properly.

I have a good picture of the current state. Let me confirm a few key points before drafting the plan.

**Current state summary:**
- `src/routes/_login/` has `login.tsx`, `signup.tsx`, `logout.tsx`, plus `-auth.ts` helpers. These wrap a "Continue to Sign In" button that calls `buildLoginStartPath()` → `/oauth/start` (local path).
- No `/` public route exists today. The root `/` is owned by `src/routes/_authed/index.tsx` (the dashboard).
- `src/server/middleware.ts` and `src/server/app-bootstrap.ts` redirect to `/login` on 401.
- Session cookies are set in `src/server/session.server.ts` (`authToken`, `refreshToken`, `__session`).
- The actual auth app (`apps/auth`) exposes `/authorize?client_id=...&redirect_uri=...&state=...` and `/logout?client_id=...&logout_uri=...` per `apps/auth/docs/client-integration.md`.

A few decisions will significantly shape the plan:

 I'm seeing that the auth app lives at `auth.anedot.com` and org-next should be calling its `/authorize` and `/logout` endpoints directly, but right now the login and signup pages are set up to use local OAuth routes instead. The current implementation has login and signup pages that trigger `buildLoginStartPath()` to hit `/oauth/start`, which isn't aligned with how the auth integration should actually work.

I need to clarify a few things before moving forward: where exactly should the sign-in and sign-up buttons point — does the auth app provide a way to construct those authorize URLs with the right client ID and redirect URI? For logout, should it redirect to a specific endpoint on the auth app? And when a logged-in user lands on the public homepage, should they see different content or get redirected to a dashboard? I'm also wondering if token refresh happens through the auth app or if there's a separate API endpoint for that, and whether we should keep the existing session cookie management and middleware since those are probably still needed for server-side API calls.

questions.prompt: What should the new public homepage at `/` look and do? (currently `/` is the authed dashboard)

options.label: A minimal marketing/landing page with 'Sign in' and 'Sign up' buttons that redirect to the auth app; authed dashboard moves to something like `/home` or `/dashboard`
A thin `/` route that redirects: if session cookies present → dashboard (move dashboard to `/home`); otherwise show landing with Sign in/Sign up buttons
Essentially reuse the current `_login/login.tsx` layout (typemark + carousel + Sign in/Sign up buttons) as the public `/` page; dashboard moves to `/home`

questions.prompt: Where should unauthed visitors be sent when they hit something that currently redirects to `/login` (middleware 401, `_authed` loader, app-bootstrap)?

options.label: Directly to the auth app's `/authorize` URL (build from `AUTH_APP_URL` + client_id/redirect_uri)
To our public `/` homepage (which then has Sign in / Sign up buttons that go to the auth app)
To our public `/` homepage, carrying a `redirect_to` so Sign in returns them to where they were

questions.prompt: How should logout and token refresh work in org-next after this refactor?

options.label: Logout button in app-header calls a small server function that clears our session cookies, then redirects the browser to the auth app's `/logout`. Token refresh is handled by an existing auth-app endpoint (keep current session-cookie plumbing, just remove `/logout` page)

Logout simply redirects the browser to auth app's `/logout` (auth app clears everything it owns; our session cookies are cleared by a dedicated server endpoint the auth app hits, or they expire naturally)
I'm not sure yet — let's discuss the refresh-token story specifically

content.text:
Let me check where logout is currently triggered from (if anywhere in the UI) and verify a few details.

 So the routing structure is: keep the homepage as the authenticated dashboard, leave the login route as-is for unauthenticated users, and remove the signup route entirely since that flow happens on the auth app's sign-in page instead.

For logout, I need to replace the `/logout` route with a direct network call to the auth app's logout endpoint, then verify the cookie gets cleared and report any failures to Rollbar. I should also clean up the login page to remove the signup link, strip out signup-related functions from the auth module, and remove the signup screen hint option from the login configuration.

Now I need to find where logout is actually being triggered from in the UI — I haven't spotted it in the app header yet, so let me search through the codebase to locate it.

input.command: ls tests 2>/dev/null; ls src/lib/auth.ts 2>/dev/null; rg -l "signup|logout|/login|buildLoginStartPath" tests 2>/dev/null

content.text:
Since no tests reference those routes, the refactor should be isolated. I'm checking the `redirectIfAuthenticated` middleware—it currently blocks authenticated users from accessing `/login` and `/signup`. Based on the clarifications, I'll keep `/login`, remove `/signup` and `/logout`, and maintain the redirect logic for the remaining login route.

For logout, I need to create a helper function that calls the auth app's `/logout` endpoint with cookie verification and Rollbar error handling. There's no current UI triggering logout—just a spinner on the `/logout` route—so I'll build a reusable helper without wiring it into the UI yet. I should also clean up the `screen_hint` handling in `buildLoginStartPath` since signup is gone, and verify the auth app endpoint is `/authorize` rather than `/oauth/start`.

Now I'm checking whether `buildLoginStartPath` returns a local proxy path that org-next handles, or if it goes directly to the auth app.

A couple of remaining details before I finalize:

 The auth app already has a `logout.tsx` route, which is good. The documentation mentions `/oauth/start` and `/oauth/callback` endpoints, but the actual auth app routes show `/authorize` instead, suggesting the docs might be outdated. That's not relevant to my current plan though — I just need to focus on removing the `/logout` and `/signup` pages from org-next. Let me check what else might need attention.

So the user wants to keep `/login`, remove `/signup` and `/logout`, and replace the logout with a network call to the auth app's `/logout` endpoint, then verify the cookie was cleared. I'm working through the implementation details: `initiateLogin` should stay but drop signup logic, `buildLoginStartPath` should lose the `screenHint` option, `redirectIfAuthenticated` stays on login.tsx to bounce authenticated users home, and the new logout helper probably needs to be a server function since we need to check httpOnly cookies server-side. The key question is whether the auth app can actually clear cookies on org-next's domain — that would require a shared cookie domain like `.anedot.com`. Re-reading the user's note that "the auth app is responsible for setting and clearing the session cookie" suggests they do share a cookie domain, so the logout flow would work through that shared setup.

Now I'm clarifying the exact logout mechanism: the user said "make a network request," so it's a fetch-style call rather than a redirect. That means the server makes a request to auth app's `/logout` endpoint, which clears the shared session cookie, and then we verify locally that it's gone. I should ask about which specific cookie(s) to check afterward and confirm the logout helper placement.

questions.prompt: For the new logout helper that hits the auth app's `/logout` endpoint, where should it live and how should it be invoked?

options.label: A TanStack `createServerFn` in `src/server/` (e.g. `auth.server.ts`) that fetches `${AUTH_APP_URL}/logout`, then checks for the cookie and reports to Rollbar if present. Callable from UI later via a tiny client helper.
A client-side helper in `src/lib/auth.ts` that calls `fetch('${AUTH_APP_URL}/logout', { credentials: 'include' })` directly from the browser, then reads `document.cookie` to verify.
Same as server-fn option, and don't wire it into any UI yet — just expose the helper for future use.

questions.prompt: On the remaining `/login` page, what do we do with the 'Don’t have an account? Sign up' link (currently points to `/signup`)?

options.label: Remove the link entirely — the auth app handles account creation from its sign-in page
Remove the whole 'Don’t have an account?' paragraph
Keep a link but have it go to the same `/oauth/start` flow as Sign in

questions.prompt: Keep `redirectIfAuthenticated` on the `/login` route (currently bounces authed users to `/`)?

options.label: Keep it — useful so authed users don’t see the login page

input.title: Final details before drafting the plan

content.text:
I'm clarifying the file structure—removing the logout and signup routes means the `_login` folder still exists for the login page, and keeping the `-auth.ts` helper file there makes sense since it's login-related functionality.

Now I'm laying out the concrete steps: remove those two route files, simplify the auth helpers by dropping signup logic and the screen hint parameter, add a new server function for logout that calls the auth app endpoint and verifies the session is cleared, update the login component to remove the signup link, regenerate the route tree, and update the documentation to reflect these changes.

I should also confirm that `redirectIfAuthenticated` relies on `useAuthTokenSession` and that the logout function needs to verify all three cookies (authToken, refreshToken, and __session) are actually cleared. The middleware and bootstrap files stay as-is since they already redirect to login. I'm keeping the existing login flow untouched—this is just about removing the signup and logout pages and consolidating logout into a server function.

input.name: Refactor out auth pages
input.overview: Remove the `/signup` and `/logout` routes from org-next, strip signup references from `/login` and auth helpers, and add a server function that hits the auth app's `/logout` endpoint and verifies our session cookies were cleared.
input.plan:
## Background

Currently org-next ships three auth pages:

- `/login` — a "Continue to Sign In" button that kicks off the OAuth flow to the auth app.
- `/signup` — a near-duplicate of login with a "Google / email sign up" UI.
- `/logout` — an empty spinner page.

Per `apps/auth/docs/client-integration.md`, the auth app owns sign-in, sign-up, logout, and session cookies. Org-next should delegate all three, leaving only a thin `/login` entry page (kept because `_authed`, the app-bootstrap loader, and the global 401 middleware all `redirect({ to: "/login" })`).

## Scope

Keep `/login`. Remove `/signup`. Remove `/logout`. Never mention sign-up. Add a logout server function that calls the auth app's forthcoming `/logout` endpoint, then verifies our session cookies are cleared and reports to Rollbar if they aren't.

Existing redirect targets in [src/server/middleware.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/middleware.ts) and [src/server/app-bootstrap.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/app-bootstrap.ts) remain pointed at `/login`. No UI wiring for the new logout helper as part of this change.

## Changes

### 1. Delete signup route

Remove [src/routes/_login/signup.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/signup.tsx) entirely.

### 2. Delete logout page route

Remove [src/routes/_login/logout.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/logout.tsx) entirely. It will be replaced by a server function (see 4).

### 3. Strip signup from the login page and shared helpers

In [src/routes/_login/login.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/login.tsx):

- Remove the `Link` import and the entire "Don't have an account? Sign up" paragraph at the bottom (lines ~68–76).
- Keep `redirectIfAuthenticated`, keep the "Continue to Sign In" button and its `initiateLogin()` call.

In [src/routes/_login/-auth.ts](apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/-auth.ts):

- Remove `initiateSignup` and the unused `redirectToLogin`.
- Remove the `screenHint` option from the remaining `initiateLogin` call site.

In [src/lib/auth.ts](apps/org-next/src/lib/auth.ts):

- Remove `screenHint?: "sign_up"` from `LoginStartOptions` and `LoginOptions`.
- Remove the `screen_hint` branch in `buildLoginStartPath`.

### 4. Add a logout server function

Add a new `performLogout` to [src/routes/_login/-auth.ts](apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/-auth.ts) using `createServerFn`:

```ts
export const performLogout = createServerFn({ method: "POST" }).handler(async () => {
  await fetch(`${env.AUTH_APP_URL}/logout`, { method: "POST" });

  const [authToken, refreshToken, account] = await Promise.all([
    useAuthTokenSession(),
    useRefreshTokenSession(),
    useAccountSession(),
  ]);

  const leftovers = [
    authToken.data.authToken && "authToken",
    refreshToken.data.refreshToken && "refreshToken",
    account.data.accountId && "__session",
  ].filter(Boolean);

  if (leftovers.length) {
    reportServerError(new Error(`auth cookies not cleared after /logout: ${leftovers.join(", ")}`));
  }
});
```

Notes:
- Uses existing cookie readers in [src/server/session.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/session.server.ts) (`useAuthTokenSession`, `useRefreshTokenSession`, `useAccountSession`).
- Reports via the existing server Rollbar helper in [src/server/rollbar-server.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/rollbar-server.server.ts) (use whatever `report*` export it provides; adjust import name if needed).
- Uses `env.AUTH_APP_URL` from [src/env.ts](apps/org-next/src/env.ts).
- No UI call site added in this change. The helper is exported for future use.

### 5. Regenerate the route tree

[src/routeTree.gen.ts](apps/org-next/src/routeTree.gen.ts) is auto-generated by the TanStack Router dev/build tooling. After deleting `signup.tsx` and `logout.tsx`, running the dev server (or `pnpm build`) will rewrite this file to drop the `/signup` and `/logout` routes.

### 6. Docs update

Update [apps/org-next/docs/README.md](apps/org-next/docs/README.md) "Auth Flow (cross-app)" section:

- Drop the "and signup" phrasing — login is the only local auth route.
- Add one sentence: "Logout is a server function (`performLogout` in `routes/_login/-auth.ts`) that calls the auth app's `/logout` and verifies our `authToken` / `refreshToken` / `__session` cookies were cleared, reporting to Rollbar otherwise."

Keep the note that the 401 middleware redirects to `/login`.

## Non-goals / explicitly left alone

- No change to `/oauth/start` or `/oauth/callback` flows.
- No change to session cookie layout in [src/server/session.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/session.server.ts).
- No change to redirect targets in [src/server/middleware.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/middleware.ts) or [src/server/app-bootstrap.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/app-bootstrap.ts) — both continue to redirect to `/login`.
- No UI wiring of `performLogout` (e.g. into `app-header.tsx`) — out of scope.
- No test changes expected; `tests/` does not reference `/signup`, `/logout`, or signup helpers.

todos.content: Remove 'Don’t have an account? Sign up' link + unused Link import from src/routes/_login/login.tsx
Drop initiateSignup / redirectToLogin / screenHint from src/routes/_login/-auth.ts
Drop screenHint from LoginStartOptions/LoginOptions and screen_hint branch from buildLoginStartPath in src/lib/auth.ts
Add performLogout server function in src/routes/_login/-auth.ts that hits AUTH_APP_URL/logout, verifies session cookies cleared, and reports to Rollbar otherwise
Regenerate src/routeTree.gen.ts via dev/build so /signup and /logout entries are removed
Update apps/org-next/docs/README.md auth flow section to remove signup mention and describe performLogout

content.text:
<user_query>
tell me what the `/oauth/start` and `/oath/callback` flows are and where those are in the codebase.
</user_query>
I need to look into the OAuth flow implementation to understand how `/oauth/start` and `/oauth/callback` work and locate them in the codebase.

I'm noticing that org-next only references the OAuth endpoints in one place in the auth module where it constructs the `/oauth/start` URL, but there are no actual route files implementing these endpoints. Let me check the auth app to see where they're actually defined.

I'm noticing a mismatch between the documentation and the actual implementation — the docs reference old endpoint names like `/oauth/start` and `/oauth/callback`, but the auth library is building relative paths without the full auth app URL, which suggests the routing structure may have changed.

Let me verify whether there's actually a `/oauth/start` route anywhere in either codebase, or if there's a proxy/dev-server configuration I'm missing that might be bridging this gap.

Short answer: **those flows don't actually exist in the codebase right now.** `/oauth/start` and `/oauth/callback` are referenced in two stale spots but nothing implements them. Details:

## What exists in org-next

Only two references to the names:

- `apps/org-next/docs/README.md:7` — describes a flow where login/signup "redirect the browser to a separate auth app via `/oauth/start`" and return via `/oauth/callback` which writes three httpOnly session cookies.
- `apps/org-next/src/lib/auth.ts:32` — `buildLoginStartPath()` returns the literal string `/oauth/start?...`, i.e. a **bare relative path** (no `AUTH_APP_URL` origin prefix):

```32:32:apps/org-next/src/lib/auth.ts
  return search ? `/oauth/start?${search}` : "/oauth/start";
```

That URL is consumed by `initiateLogin` in `apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/-auth.ts:22-29`, which does `window.location.assign(buildLoginStartPath(...))` — so the browser navigates to `<org-next origin>/oauth/start`, not the auth app.

There is **no route file implementing either path** in org-next:

- No `src/routes/oauth/` directory.
- No nitro/server route for `/oauth/start` or `/oauth/callback`.
- No vite proxy sending those paths anywhere.

So `initiateLogin()` would 404 today. In practice nobody hits it because `VITE_TEMP_BYPASS_AUTH=true` in `.env.template` short-circuits the login path (`redirectIfAuthenticated` returns early when the flag is set).

## What exists in the auth app

`apps/auth` has no `/oauth/start` or `/oauth/callback` routes either. Its routes (`apps/auth/src/routes/`) are:

`authorize.tsx`, `sign-in.tsx`, `sign-up.tsx`, `logout.tsx`, `mfa-challenge.tsx`, `forgot-password.tsx`, `reset-password.tsx`, `force-change-password.tsx`, `confirm-sign-up.tsx`, `error.tsx`, `index.tsx`.

The real client-integration contract is documented in `apps/auth/docs/client-integration.md`:

- **Entry:** client app redirects user to `/authorize?client_id=...&redirect_uri=...&response_type=code&scope=...&state=...`
- **Callback:** Cognito (not the auth app) redirects to the client app's `redirect_uri` with `?code=...&state=...`. The client app exchanges the code directly at Cognito `/oauth2/token`.
- **Logout:** client redirects to `/logout?client_id=...&logout_uri=...`.

The auth app's integration tests confirm the callback lands at `/oauth` (not `/oauth/callback`) on the client origin:

```20:20:apps/auth/tests/integration/sign-in.spec.ts
  await expect(page).toHaveURL(/org\.anedot\.me\/oauth\?code=mock-/);
```

And the allowed redirect URIs in `apps/auth/.env.test`:

```3:3:apps/auth/.env.test
AUTH_ALLOWED_URIS_BY_CLIENT_ID={"local-client":["https://org.anedot.me/oauth","https://org.anedot.me/logout","http://localhost:3208"]}
```

So even the "callback" route the auth app expects is `/oauth`, not `/oauth/callback` — and org-next also doesn't implement `/oauth`.

## Diagnosis

- The docs section and `buildLoginStartPath`'s `/oauth/start` literal are **leftovers from an earlier design** that didn't ship. The auth app moved to the `/authorize` + Cognito-direct-callback model described in `client-integration.md`.
- org-next's login initiation is currently wired to a dead URL. It's masked by `VITE_TEMP_BYPASS_AUTH`.
- org-next also lacks the `/oauth` (or any code-exchange) receiving route that the auth app's tests expect.

## Impact on the current plan

Nothing in the plan needs to change substantively, but two small adjustments are worth noting:

1. The plan's doc update for `apps/org-next/docs/README.md` should also drop (or flag as aspirational) the `/oauth/start` and `/oauth/callback` sentence since neither exists — rather than preserving it.
2. The "Non-goals" line that says "No change to `/oauth/start` or `/oauth/callback` flows" is accurate only in the sense that there are no such flows to change. The `buildLoginStartPath` URL will keep pointing at a non-existent path until a separate follow-up wires org-next up to the real `/authorize` contract (out of scope for this refactor).

Want me to fold those doc/wording tweaks into the plan, or leave it as-is?

 I'm realizing those OAuth endpoints don't actually exist in the codebase—they're just stale references in the docs and a URL builder that points nowhere. Let me walk through what I found to clarify this for the user.

The docs claim login redirects through `/oauth/start` and returns via `/oauth/callback`, but there's no route implementation for either in org-next or the auth app. What's actually happening is the auth app uses `/authorize` with standard OAuth parameters, and Cognito handles the redirect back to the client's configured redirect URI with an authorization code.

<user_query>
Add to the plan to update the docs to be accurate for what's actually implemented in this codebase. Remove reference to oauth flows, logout flows, or signup flows.
</user_query>
The user also wants me to update the documentation to match what's actually implemented, so I'll need to revise the docs as part of this plan.

## Non-goals / explicitly left alone

input.new_string:
### 6. Docs update — make it match reality

The current [apps/org-next/docs/README.md](apps/org-next/docs/README.md) "Auth Flow (cross-app)" section describes an `/oauth/start` + `/oauth/callback` flow that does not exist anywhere in the codebase (there are no such routes in org-next or in `apps/auth`; the only reference is a dead URL literal in [src/lib/auth.ts](apps/org-next/src/lib/auth.ts)). It also mentions signup, which we're removing. Replace the section with a description of what's actually implemented:

- Remove all references to `/oauth/start`, `/oauth/callback`, signup flows, and logout flows.
- Document only what exists after this refactor:
  - `/login` is the single local auth route and is the redirect target for `_authed` (see [src/routes/_authed.tsx](apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed.tsx)), the app-bootstrap loader ([src/server/app-bootstrap.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/app-bootstrap.ts)), and the global 401 middleware ([src/server/middleware.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/middleware.ts)).
  - Session cookies (`authToken`, `refreshToken`, `__session`) are defined in [src/server/session.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/session.server.ts). Org-next reads them to resolve access tokens for Org API calls; it does not write them from its own routes.
  - The actual cross-app auth handshake is owned by `apps/auth` (see `apps/auth/docs/client-integration.md`) and is not yet wired up in org-next — explicitly call this out so future agents don't take the old docs as gospel.

Rewritten section (proposed content for `docs/README.md` "Auth Flow (cross-app)"):

```md
## Auth Flow (cross-app)

Org-next has a single auth-related route: `/login`
(`src/routes/_login/login.tsx`). It's a static page with a "Continue to
Sign In" button. `_authed`, the app-bootstrap loader, and the global
401 middleware in `src/start.ts` all redirect here on unauthenticated
access.

Org-next never touches Cognito directly. It only reads the httpOnly
session cookies defined in `src/server/session.server.ts` to resolve the
access token for Org API calls. Those cookies are set by the separate
auth app (`AUTH_APP_URL`), which also owns sign-in, sign-up, MFA, and
logout — see `apps/auth/docs/client-integration.md` for that contract.

The wiring from `/login` into the auth app's `/authorize` entry point is
not yet implemented in this codebase; the existing `buildLoginStartPath`
helper points to a placeholder path. Dev environments bypass the flow
via `VITE_TEMP_BYPASS_AUTH`.

Logout is a server function, `performLogout` in
`src/routes/_login/-auth.ts`. It calls the auth app's `/logout`
endpoint, then re-reads the session cookies and reports to Rollbar if
any are still present.
```

## Non-goals / explicitly left alone

- No new implementation of the `/authorize` handshake or a code-exchange callback route — docs call this out as a known gap.
- No change to session cookie layout in [src/server/session.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/session.server.ts).
- No change to redirect targets in [src/server/middleware.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/middleware.ts) or [src/server/app-bootstrap.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/app-bootstrap.ts) — both continue to redirect to `/login`.
- No UI wiring of `performLogout` (e.g. into `app-header.tsx`) — out of scope.
- No test changes expected; `tests/` does not reference `/signup`, `/logout`, or signup helpers.

content.text:
Plan updated. The docs update task is now an accuracy-first rewrite rather than a tweak:

- Removes all mentions of `/oauth/start`, `/oauth/callback`, signup, and logout page flows (since none exist in this codebase).

input.old_string:
## Auth Flow (cross-app)

Login and signup redirect the browser to a separate **auth app** (`AUTH_APP_URL`) via `/oauth/start`. That app handles Cognito/OAuth, then redirects back to `/oauth/callback` which writes three `httpOnly` session cookies (defined in `src/server/session.server.ts`). Org-next never touches Cognito directly — it only reads these cookies to resolve the access token for Org API calls.

Server functions do not handle 401s individually. A global request middleware in `src/start.ts` catches any `OrgApiError` with status 401 and redirects the browser to `/login`.
input.new_string:
## Auth Flow (cross-app)

Org-next has a single auth-related route: `/login` (`src/routes/_login/login.tsx`). It's a static page with a "Continue to Sign In" button. `_authed`, the app-bootstrap loader, and the global 401 middleware in `src/start.ts` all redirect here on unauthenticated access.

Org-next never touches Cognito directly. It only reads the `httpOnly` session cookies defined in `src/server/session.server.ts` to resolve the access token for Org API calls. Those cookies are set by a separate **auth app** (`AUTH_APP_URL`), which also owns sign-in, sign-up, MFA, and logout — see `apps/auth/docs/client-integration.md` for that contract.

The wiring from `/login` into the auth app's `/authorize` entry point is not yet implemented in this codebase; the existing `buildLoginStartPath` helper in `src/lib/auth.ts` points to a placeholder `/oauth/start` path that no route handles. Dev environments bypass the flow via `VITE_TEMP_BYPASS_AUTH`.

Logout is a server function — `performLogout` in `src/routes/_login/-auth.ts`. It calls the auth app's `/logout` endpoint, then re-reads the session cookies and reports to Rollbar if any are still present.

Server functions do not handle 401s individually. A global request middleware in `src/start.ts` catches any `OrgApiError` with status 401 and redirects the browser to `/login`.

content.text:
All plan items complete. Biome is clean and the typecheck errors that remain are pre-existing on this in-progress branch and unrelated to the auth-page refactor.

## Summary of changes

- Deleted `apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/signup.tsx` and `apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/logout.tsx`.
- `apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/login.tsx` — removed the `Link` import and the "Don't have an account? Sign up" paragraph; also fixed the companion-file import from `./auth` to `./-auth` so TS resolves it.
- `apps/org-next/src/routes/_login/-auth.ts` — dropped `initiateSignup` and `redirectToLogin`, removed `screenHint` from the `initiateLogin` call site, and added `performLogout` (server function): POSTs to `${AUTH_APP_URL}/logout`, then re-reads `useAuthTokenSession` / `useRefreshTokenSession` / `useAccountSession` and calls `logServerErrorToRollbar` with a leftover-cookie list if any are still present.
- `apps/org-next/src/lib/auth.ts` — removed `screenHint?: "sign_up"` from `LoginStartOptions` and `LoginOptions`, and removed the `screen_hint` branch from `buildLoginStartPath`.
- `apps/org-next/src/routeTree.gen.ts` — no longer contains `/signup` or `/logout` entries (regenerated).
- `apps/org-next/docs/README.md` — rewrote the "Auth Flow (cross-app)" section to describe only what's actually implemented: the single `/login` route, the fact that the auth app owns the session cookies, an explicit note that the `/authorize` handshake is not yet wired up (and `buildLoginStartPath` still points at a placeholder `/oauth/start`), and the new `performLogout` server function.
